name: Una Mae Carlisle
description: American jazz singer, pianist, and songwriter (1915–1956)
abstract: Una Mae Carlisle (December 26, 1915 – November 7, 1956) was an American jazz singer, pianist, and songwriter.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Salute to Fats Waller

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't know her but she had a tremendous sensitivity and she sounded good on the date

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: Una Mae Carlisle was playing piano for me, and augmented the band to twelve pieces

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: There's Una Mae Carlisle and Johnny Bradford, teamed with you on a song, 'Who's Kissing Who.' ... Yeah. Una Mae Carlisle, yeah. We did -- oh, my goodness. She was a great artist, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Una Mae Carlisle was playing in a club in St. Louis. I had known her from a club in Harlem and she was also in Paris in the 30's when I was there.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Una Mae, she's figured not very well known about... [INTERVIEWEE] She was very -- no. Una Mae Carlisle?

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: "Because of You was written by a colored girl from Ohio. I can't think of her name."
